health_zone_table.basis: SitRep41 Table 2 (24 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1/province totals 1155/304. No new affected health zone is reported; the affected-zone count remains 34/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. PDF sha256 656822f7d31650f2b46c602c7decd620fd7ebb4b9f92d5e0bde730b8184182c2.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1138 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation; all 304 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep41 section 4.3: Ituri 134 collected/analyzed / 34 positive; Nord-Kivu 117 received, 97 analyzed / 3 positive (20 still under analysis); Sud-Kivu 6 collected/analyzed / 0 positive. Positivity uses 37 positives over 237 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 385 patients (205 confirmed, 180 suspected) at 74.9% global occupancy.
Ituri isolation occupancy was 85.3% with 313 patients isolated at end day; Nord-Kivu was 57.3% with 59 and Sud-Kivu 29.5% with 13.
One escape was recorded in Ituri during the cycle; 16 recovered exits were recorded, all in Ituri.
highlights: 37 new confirmed cases including 5 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-24, in Ituri (34 including 4 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (3 including 1 death).
New confirmed cases were distributed across Rwampara (12), Bunia (10), Nyankunde (5), Mongbwalu (4), Bambu (2) and Tchomia (1) in Ituri, and Butembo (2), Katwa (1) in Nord-Kivu.
8 additional deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients in Ituri (Bunia 4, Mongbwalu 2, Nyankunde 1, Rwampara 1), bringing confirmed deaths to 304.
16 patients were declared recovered in Ituri after negative control tests, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 138.
laboratory: SitRep41 reports Ituri 134 samples collected and analyzed with 34 positive (25.0%).
Nord-Kivu received 117 samples, analyzed 97, with 3 positive (3.1%) and 20 still under analysis; Sud-Kivu collected and analyzed 6, all negative.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 41 reports through 2026-06-24 and was published on the INSP WordPress site on 2026-06-25T22:03:56Z (post 25054).
The outbreak remains reported across 3 provinces and 34 of 104 health zones; no new affected health zone is reported in this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1054 of 1155 DRC confirmed cases (91.3%).
Nord-Kivu stands at 98 confirmed cases and keeps the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.1%, led by Oicha (66.7%), Kyondo (66.7%), Katwa (60.6%), and Beni (60.0%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 903 alerts were reported and 744 investigated, producing an 82.4% investigation rate; Nord-Kivu investigated 507 of 665 alerts (76.2%).
154 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 40 deceased validated alerts.
9,305 contacts were under follow-up and 7,368 were seen, producing 79.2% follow-up coverage.
311 new contacts were listed across 9 Ituri health zones; 99 contacts completed 21-day follow-up successfully, including 79 in Nord-Kivu and 20 in Ituri.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 94; new alerts 809; 311 new contacts listed across 9 Ituri health zones; 99 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (79 Nord-Kivu, 20 Ituri).

challenges.finding: Ituri CTE occupancy stood at 85.3% with an estimated gap of about 20 isolation centers.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction and expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 79.2% versus the 95% target (a 15.8 pp gap).
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet precisely ventilated by health zone.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, duplicate cleanup, field verification, and reclassification.

challenges.finding: The SitRep41 challenge table reports 78 health-worker infections and 18 deaths (CFR 23.1%).
challenges.response: Reinforce IPC/WASH supplies (PPE, chlorine), supervision, and training.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu carries a laboratory sample backlog and late diagnosis amid a 54.1% provincial CFR, with 20 samples still under analysis.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 311 new contacts were listed across 9 Ituri health zones; 99 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (79 Nord-Kivu, 20 Ituri).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones
Protect Ituri isolation capacity (85.3% occupancy) and close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (79.2% vs 95% target) and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog (20 samples pending) given the 54.1% provincial CFR

preserved_conflicts: SitRep41 headline is DRC-only (1155 confirmed / 304 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,155 confirmed total is marked re-actualized after DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization across health zones, laboratories, and care structures; treat the +37 confirmed and +13 confirmed deaths as official revised deltas, not raw incident-line-list deltas.
SitRep41 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.

Ituri recorded 8 additional deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (Bunia 4, Mongbwalu 2, Nyankunde 1, Rwampara 1); with the 5 deaths among the 37 new confirmed cases, the confirmed-death total advances by 13 to 304.
Daily suspected cases (154) and the 40 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard, carried forward unchanged from SitRep40; the Uganda dashboard/API was unreachable this cycle, so this is a carried-forward anchor and not a fresh confirmation (INSP reports DRC only).

The France imported confirmed case (a reanimation physician who worked the Bunia response for ALIMA and developed signs in transit) is an exportation/importation signal, not a DRC denominator addition.
An INSP expert deployed to Ituri died in Kinshasa on 24 June 2026 after a hypertensive episode; two samples were negative for MVE, so this is not counted as a confirmed case or death.
North-Kivu patient-movement figures (Table 6) are dated 2026-06-23 because 24 June data were unavailable at consolidation; the headline counts and the health-zone table remain 24 June.

The SitRep highlights paragraph dates the 37 new confirmed cases 23 June 2026, while the report title, Table 1, and all detail tables date them 24 June 2026; treated as 2026-06-24 per the table and title basis.

The 90-day RDC-Uganda cross-border response plan (joint laboratory and clinical-care capacity, starting with the strategic Aru zone) is operational readiness and coordination evidence, not proof of realized capacity.

review_notes: SitRep #041 (report date 2026-06-24; INSP WordPress post 25054 published 2026-06-25T22:03:56Z; embedded PDF media 25055). PDF sha256 656822f7d31650f2b46c602c7decd620fd7ebb4b9f92d5e0bde730b8184182c2, 11 pages. Title N041/MVB_24/06/2026; treated as report-date 2026-06-24.

DRC headline 1155 confirmed / 304 confirmed deaths (CFR 26.3%); +37 confirmed and +13 confirmed deaths vs SitRep40. The 37 new confirmed (Ituri 34, Nord-Kivu 3) carried 5 deaths; a further 8 deaths occurred among previously confirmed Ituri CTE patients, totalling the +13.
The 1,155 total is flagged re-actualized after DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization; deltas are official revised deltas.
The affected-zone count remains 34/104. No new affected health zone is reported, so no new map geometry/centroid is required this cycle. The zone set is identical to SitRep40.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1155 confirmed / 304 deaths: 1138 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 304 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 138 DRC cumulative (+16, all Ituri; 129 Ituri / 7 Nord-Kivu / 2 Sud-Kivu). Uganda anchor carried forward unchanged at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (dashboard unreachable this cycle), so country-scope is 1175 confirmed / 306 deaths / 149 recovered.
Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 134 collected/analyzed, 34 positive; Nord-Kivu 117 received, 97 analyzed, 3 positive, 20 pending; Sud-Kivu 6 collected/analyzed, 0 positive.

Operational data captured: 903 alerts (744 investigated, 82.4%; Nord-Kivu 507/665, 76.2%), 154 suspected cases of the day (40 deceased validated alerts), 9,305 contacts under follow-up (79.2%), 385 in isolation (205 confirmed / 180 suspected, 74.9% occupancy), 311 new contacts (9 Ituri zones), PoC activation 86.8% (59/68) with 264,124 travelers passed.

France imported case and the Kinshasa INSP-expert death (two negative MVE samples) are recorded as context only, not added to DRC counts. North-Kivu patient-movement figures are dated 23 June (24 June unavailable). Promoted as table facts only.
